scope_boundary: Public-safe metadata import over three existing local .rfa archive observations only: digest consensus for one 100,000,000-byte decoded artifact, archive-byte ratios, rfa verify summaries, and NEURAL0 inspect metadata. The source checkout was dirty and the import did not copy dataset bytes, archive bytes, or rerun compression from source input.
transition_reason: The artifact import is useful because it surfaces concrete local RankFold archive observations, decoded digest consensus, verifier summaries, and NEURAL0 metadata that were previously hidden in local project output. It remains a read-only metadata import from existing artifacts, not a fresh benchmark or codec-correctness result, so it blocks promotion for compression performance, deterministic decoder correctness, downstream utility, fallback execution, deployed compression, benchmark, or chapter-core claims.
required_artifacts: fresh public-safe compression run from source input
negative_results: dataset and archive bytes are not copied into the repository
record states that compression was not rerun from source input
non-claims block codec-correctness, benchmark, downstream-utility, fallback-execution, and deployed-compression claims
no chapter core claim is promoted
downgrade_triggers: rfa verify summary no longer reports 1 OK and 0 failed for each observation
metadata import described as a fresh compression benchmark
support-state promotion without a separate accepted transition
promotion_burden: Move above argument only for a narrowed RankFold compression claim after a fresh public-safe compression run from source input, clean or archived source state, corpus provenance, decoder correctness review, downstream utility/fallback tests, and independent review are recorded with a separate accepted transition.
limitations: read-only metadata import from existing local artifacts
no dataset bytes or archive bytes copied into this repository
no compression rerun from source input
no deterministic decoder proof beyond recorded decoded digest observations
reviewer_independence: local maintainer-agent review only; not an independent compression, codec-correctness, benchmark, or downstream-utility audit
acceptance_blockers: no fresh public-safe compression run from source input
no clean or archived source-project state
no downstream utility or fallback-execution test
non_claims: does not promote RankFold/NeuralFold above argument

does not prove RankFold codec correctness, deterministic decoder correctness beyond recorded local decoded digest observations, compression from source input, benchmark performance, downstream utility, fallback execution, or deployed compression behavior
does not create an upward support-state transition
does not promote the RankFold chapter core claim
